enterpriseWatchlist.name: Together AI

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Cerebras
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Public (CBRS on Nasdaq; IPO priced May 13, 2026 at $185/share; $5.55B raised; $56.4B fully diluted valuation; began trading May 14, 2026 with early prints implying ~$100B market cap)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: WSE-3 wafer-scale chip; $510M 2025 revenue; $10B OpenAI compute deal; priced at $185 (well above $150-160 revised range); raised $5.55B — largest AI chip IPO in history; Day 1 trading (May 14) showed early prints near $350/share (~$100B implied market cap); validates pure-play AI infrastructure as public market category; NVIDIA alternatives now investor-credible at scale

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Baseten
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Serverless ML model deployment; $150M Series D Apr 2026; abstracts GPU fleet for enterprise inference workloads; competes with Modal Labs, Parasail

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Cursor
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series C (in talks, $2B raise at $50B+ valuation, Apr 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: AI-native code editor with 400K+ paying developers; in talks to raise $2B led by a16z + NVIDIA at $50B+ valuation (Apr 19, 2026); signals developer tools reaching infrastructure-tier valuations; direct competitor to GitHub Copilot and new agent coding frameworks

enterpriseWatchlist.name: MatX
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Ex-Google TPU founders; $500M Series B led by Jane Street + Situational Awareness (Leopold Aschenbrenner); targeting 10x LLM training perf over H100; TSMC-fabbed, first shipments 2027; credible non-NVIDIA training chip challenger

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Lambda Labs
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: 'Superintelligence Cloud' positioning; $2.5B+ total capital deployed to build gigawatt-scale AI factories (10K Blackwell Ultra GPUs in KC facility); J.P. Morgan-led $1B credit facility announced May 7, 2026; competes with CoreWeave (CRWV, IPO'd) for GPU-native cloud market below hyperscalers

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Project Prometheus
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Physical AI for Industrial Manufacturing Simulation
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: $10B round at $38B valuation (closed Apr 2026, JPMorgan + BlackRock co-led)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Co-CEOs Jeff Bezos + Vik Bajaj; builds AI simulation models to automate chip fabs, automotive assembly, and aerospace manufacturing; launched with $6.2B, added $10B round at $38B val in Apr 2026; Bezos also targeting $100B acquisition fund for industrial companies; bifurcates from 'embodied robotics' into factory process simulation — $16T manufacturing TAM with <2% AI penetration

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Sunday Robotics
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Building 'Memo', a household robot entering beta later in 2026; $165M Series B at $1.15B valuation — first consumer robotics unicorn in this category; targets sub-$5K price point; competes with labor costs (cleaning services, elder care); Mountain View, CA. Category signal: if Memo ships, it validates consumer robotics as mass market (analogous to Roomba → iPhone step change)

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Nous Research / Hermes Agent
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Hermes Agent reached 160K+ GitHub stars in May 2026 -- fastest-growing open-source agent framework of the year, beating OpenClaw's star velocity at equivalent age. Key innovation: persistent cross-session memory + automatic skill file generation from successful task completions. Agents accumulate proprietary workflow knowledge as organizational IP. Distinct from orchestration-layer agents (LangGraph, CrewAI): this is the memory + self-improvement persistence layer. Signal: enterprise dev tooling adoption velocity indicates the 'agent memory' infrastructure layer is becoming a standard requirement for production agents.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Skild AI
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Growth (acquired Zebra Technologies Robotics Automation, Apr 2026)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Acquired Zebra Technologies' Robotics Automation business (Symmetry platform + Fetch Robotics AMRs) in April 2026 to create the first end-to-end AI-native warehouse automation stack. Skild's omni-bodied foundation model controls humanoids, robotic dogs, arms, and AMRs from a single AI brain -- no per-robot reprogramming. Zebra received cash + equity stake. Positions Skild as the full-stack robotics vendor for logistics: hardware-agnostic AI orchestration across all warehouse robot form factors. Category signal: physical AI for logistics is consolidating from specialized to generalist foundation models.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Rhoda AI
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Exited stealth March 2026 with $450M Series A (Khosla, Mayfield, Temasek, John Doerr) to build FutureVision -- a Direct Video Action (DVA) model pre-trained on hundreds of millions of internet videos. Rather than teleoperated trajectory data, Rhoda trains on video to learn physics, motion, and physical interaction priors, then post-trains on small amounts of robot-specific data. The DVA model continuously observes environment, predicts future states as video, converts predictions to robot actions every few hundred milliseconds in a closed loop. Key differentiation: generalization across novel environments without re-training. Watch: DVA architecture could be the foundation model approach that finally bridges the robotics reality gap.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Nscale
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Europe's largest-ever Series C (March 9, 2026), 4× valuation jump from Series B. UK/Norway-headquartered AI compute hyperscaler backed by NVIDIA, Dell, Nokia, Citadel, Jane Street, and Point72. Nick Clegg (ex-Meta VP Global Affairs, ex-EU Deputy PM) and Sheryl Sandberg join board — EU regulatory fluency as a core product differentiator. Targets regulated EU enterprises (banks, hospitals, defense) blocked from US-CLOUD-Act-governed hyperscalers by GDPR Art. 44, EU AI Act, and NIS2. Distinct from US GPU clouds (Lambda, CoreWeave) and national sovereign compute (government clusters). NVIDIA strategic investment signals Nscale is a key non-US distribution channel for Blackwell hardware.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Anthropic
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Frontier AI Model Platform (Claude API / Enterprise AI)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Pre-IPO (Confidential S-1 filed June 1, 2026; $965B valuation post $65B Series H; Nasdaq Oct 2026 target)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Confidentially filed S-1 with SEC on June 1, 2026 after closing $65B Series H led by Altimeter, Sequoia, Dragoneer. $965B post-money valuation — world's most valuable private AI company, eclipsing OpenAI's $852B for the first time. $47B ARR run-rate as of May 2026 (up from $30B in April 2026); first profitable quarter projected Q2 2026. Oct 2026 Nasdaq listing targeting $1T+ debut valuation. Enterprise signal: 72 open sales roles vs 67 research roles in the S-1 — Claude is now primarily a commercial enterprise utility. Expected to beat OpenAI to public markets by 6-12 months. Key risk for enterprise CTOs: once public, pricing power hardens — companies with >$5M annual Claude API spend face accelerating lock-in.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: NEURA Robotics
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Full-Stack Physical AI Platform (Humanoid + Mobile Robots + Cobot Arms)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series C ($1.4B, June 10, 2026; investors: NVIDIA, Amazon, Qualcomm, Tether, Bosch, Schaeffler, European Investment Bank)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Largest full-stack robotics funding round in history (June 10, 2026). Germany-based physical AI platform building humanoid robots, mobile robots, light robot arms, and sensor kits for manufacturing + supply chain. Key moat: 'NEURA Gyms' — real-world proprietary training environments for cognitive robots, making experiential training data the structural advantage analogous to Waymo's miles-driven dataset. Investor coalition (NVIDIA silicon + Amazon cloud/distribution + Qualcomm edge inference + Bosch/Schaeffler OEM customers) creates a vertically integrated route to European industrial market. EU domicile is a product feature for GDPR/NIS2-regulated European enterprise customers blocked from US-cloud-governed robots. Target: serial production to multi-million robots by 2030.

enterpriseWatchlist.name: Armada
enterpriseWatchlist.domain: Edge AI Infrastructure (Disconnected / Remote / Military AI Compute)
enterpriseWatchlist.stage: Series B ($230M, $2.2B valuation; led by 8090 Industries, BlackRock, Overmatch Ventures)
enterpriseWatchlist.notes: Builds purpose-built AI infrastructure nodes for edge environments where centralized GPU cloud is unavailable: military forward operating bases, remote industrial sites, maritime/airborne platforms. $230M Series B led by BlackRock + 8090 Industries (defense tech-focused) + Overmatch Ventures. Distinct from Lambda Labs / CoreWeave (centralized AI factories) — Armada targets constrained-compute environments where latency, air-gap, and sovereignty requirements make cloud inference impossible. Signal: defense AI and remote industrial AI inference is large enough to attract $2.2B valuations — suggests the 'last-mile AI compute' problem is now funded at infrastructure scale, not just as military R&D.
